findings.title: North Star Metric: User Engagement
findings.description: The North Star metric for SmartNotify is the Daily Active Users (DAU), which reflects the number of unique users engaging with the notification system daily. This metric is crucial as it directly correlates with user retention and satisfaction.
assumptions: User engagement is a key driver of product success.
recommendations: Monitor DAU trends weekly to assess user engagement.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of notifications that users interact with. This metric indicates the effectiveness of notifications in capturing user attention.
assumptions: Higher CTR indicates better notification relevance.
recommendations: Aim to increase CTR by optimizing notification content.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Average Notifications per User
findings.description: This metric tracks the average number of notifications received by each user. It helps in understanding user exposure to notifications and potential fatigue.
assumptions: Lower average notifications per user can reduce fatigue.
recommendations: Consider reducing the average notifications per user to minimize fatigue.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Unsubscribe Rate
findings.description: The Unsubscribe Rate measures the percentage of users who opt-out of notifications. A high unsubscribe rate indicates potential issues with notification relevance or frequency.
assumptions: A lower unsubscribe rate is indicative of user satisfaction.
recommendations: Monitor unsubscribe rates closely and investigate spikes.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: 95th Percentile Delivery Latency
findings.description: This metric tracks the 95th percentile delivery latency of notifications. It is critical to ensure timely delivery of notifications to enhance user experience.
assumptions: Lower latency improves user satisfaction with notifications.
recommendations: Reduce latency to below 200ms as per SLO.

findings.title: Gap in Measurement of User Preferences
findings.description: There is a lack of data collection regarding user preferences for notification timing and content. This gap hinders the ability to personalize notifications effectively.
assumptions: Understanding user preferences is essential for improving engagement.

evidence.excerpt: ML model should learn per-user preferences for delivery timing and grouping thresholds.

recommendations: Implement user preference surveys and tracking mechanisms.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Notification Timing Optimization
findings.description: Conduct an experiment to test the impact of different notification timings on user engagement and CTR. This will help identify optimal delivery windows.
assumptions: Notification timing significantly affects user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Run A/B tests with varied notification timings.

summary: The analysis identifies the North Star metric as Daily Active Users, with supporting KPIs including Notification CTR and Average Notifications per User. Guardrail metrics focus on Unsubscribe Rate and Delivery Latency. A significant measurement gap exists in user preference data, and an experiment opportunity is proposed to optimize notification timing.
